headline: Coface exploits data to foresee corporate failures
summary: French trade credit insurer has reinvented itself as a provider of business intelligence for the era of the polycrisis
body_text:
Bankruptcies have surged since 2020 and are set to keep rising, as higher borrowing costs, trade shocks and global volatility hit businesses that had cruised through the prior decade.

The rise in corporate insolvencies has sent jitters through the banking system — but it has been a boon for Coface, the French trade credit insurer with roots dating back to 1923, which has reinvented itself as a provider of business intelligence data for the era of the polycrisis.

Formed out of a French export credit agency and a German imperial bond company, Coface for decades provided state-backed cover against non-payment by customers in far-flung destinations.

Today Coface, now a listed company, still sells policies that say they will pay out if customers fail to pay for goods or services purchased on credit — agreed at a future date — because of an insolvency. But it has also evolved, becoming a more generalist data company which can stop claims from occurring in the first place, bringing in new
